recent_lessons: Brandon Ingram PTS T10 miss (actual 1, injury exit): Post-game news confirmed Ingram exited Game 5 with right heel inflammation after only 11 minutes. The pick object has post_game_event = 'injury_exit' annotated directly. This is an injury_event classification — not an analytical failure. However, the pick's context warrants noting: Ingram was flagged with FG_COLD (–17%), VOLATILE, and a minimum-confidence 70% pick. The post_game_event annotation confirms the heel inflammation was the causal factor, not pick selection logic.

Jokic REB T12 missed (actual 9, 43 minutes played, no injury): Despite a 9/9 H2H record at T12 and 5/5 series record, Jokic grabbed only 9 rebounds in 43 minutes in a competitive game (DEN lost by 12). The post-game news confirms no injury or restriction — this is a genuine performance variance on a high-confidence signal. The volatile REB tag and the human trim flag (post_miss_hr 71%, near-flat lift) were prescient warnings. The miss occurred in a game where MIN's Gobert also grabbed 13 rebounds — the rebounding competition from Gobert at full health in an elimination context was a structural factor the quant signal did not reflect.

Murray PTS T15 missed (actual 12, 40 minutes played, no injury): Murray finished with 12 points in 40 full minutes against an over of 15. FG_COLD (-17%) and FG_MARGIN_THIN (0%) both fired and triggered a double step-down (T25→T20→T15), but the pick still missed by 3. The human trim flag noted the compounded shooting suppression risk and VOLATILE tag with a typical miss of 5.5 below threshold. The cold shooting streak continued, delivering exactly the kind of outcome the dual flags were warning against. The step-down rules reduced exposure but did not eliminate it.

Brunson PTS T20 missed (actual 17, 29 minutes played) — injury exit confirmed. Brunson exited to the locker room in Q3 with an apparent left ankle injury, limiting him to 29 minutes. The pick had FG_MARGIN_NEG stepped from T25→T20, which was correct given available data. This is an injury_event — no selection error. The post_game_event field on the pick object also annotates this.

Tatum AST T4 missed (actual 3, 29 minutes played) — injury exit confirmed. Tatum exited Game 6 with an apparent calf injury and played only 29 minutes. His 3 AST fell 1 short of the T4 pick value. The VOLATILE+T6 skip correctly stepped the pick from T6 to T4, and the pick was sound analytically. The injury exit — not selection logic — caused the miss. The CLV warning (ast_lost_close, -3.07pp) was a real-time market signal that the market was already pricing in some uncertainty.

Brown REB T4 missed dramatically (actual 1, 28 minutes played in a game BOS lost by 13). Brown played only 28 minutes in a 13-point road loss. His 1 rebound against a T4 pick is a notable shortfall, but the post-game news confirms no injury exit — he played 28 minutes. The competitive game context (PHI won by 13) and the road BOS context under PHI suppressor appears to have severely compressed Brown's rebounding output. The human verdict was 'keep' with a trim reason noting the FG_MARGIN_NEG and volatile combination — but the REB prop had no FG_MARGIN flag and was independently evaluated. The PHI suppressor's mechanism (scheme-based scoring disruption) appears to have extended into Brown's rebounding game through fewer offensive possessions and disrupted game flow.

Derrick White AST T2 missed (actual 1, 32 minutes played, no injury exit confirmed). White played a full 32 minutes with no mid-game exit despite the knee contusion. His 1 assist missed the T2 threshold. The web recap confirms this is performance variance — no restriction observed. The H2H 9/9 and series 5/5 signals were very strong, and the 76% confidence was appropriate, but 1 in 10 games naturally produces a miss for a pick at this rate. The CLV warning (ast_lost_close, -2.82pp) was again active on this pick, suggesting the market was pricing in risk the system's quantitative signal did not fully capture. The knee contusion's effect on aggressive ball-handling — as noted in the human trim — is a plausible mechanism.

Harden 3PM T2 missed (actual 1) in a 44-minute OT game despite a 5/5 series hit rate and 90% quant overall. The cross-season dossier update explicitly flags Harden 3PM playoff suppression: 5/5 prior playoffs below RS career, trajectory net_down (-0.88 → -2.39), PTS dossier CAUTION confirmed by trajectory. The analyst applied the 80% non-iron-floor cap correctly but did not apply a dossier-level deduction for the 3PM cross-season CAUTION signal, treating it as advisory only. In future, the playoff 3PM suppression for Harden should apply a mechanical tier-step-down (T2→T1) rather than advisory-only treatment, consistent with how other CAUTION signals trigger steps.

Amen Thompson AST T4 missed (actual 3) in a 45-minute blowout loss (LAL won by 20). The game context is a LAL blowout — on the losing side, offensive rhythm is disrupted and role definition compresses. The AST pick was 72% confidence, reflecting appropriate LAL suppressor conservatism, but the blowout game script on the losing HOU side was an additional structural suppressor that was not fully priced in. The CLV warning (ast_lost_close, live CLV dropped -2.21pp from morning) was available and not applied. In blowout losses, AST picks for the losing team are subject to the same secondary-scorer game-script compression as PTS — even primary-level AST producers see sequences cut short when the game gets out of hand.

Jabari Smith Jr. 3PM T2 and PTS T10 both missed due to his left ankle injury exit (confirmed post_game_event = injury_exit). Both are classified as injury events — no analytical lesson applies to pick selection logic for either.
recent_reinforcements: Blowout-side scoring step-downs were well-calibrated: The NYK vs ATL game ended 140-89 (51-point blowout). The Analyst stepped down KAT PTS to T15 (hit at 12, miss) and Anunoby PTS to T15 (hit at 29), and Brunson PTS to T20 — the Brunson pick missed due to an in-game ankle injury, not the blowout, while the ATL home picks (Johnson PTS T10, McCollum PTS T10) both hit cleanly. Conservative tier selection in blowout-risk games proved correct across this context.

Mandatory FG_COLD and FG_MARGIN step-down rules protected the system from overreach on multiple PTS props. Jokic PTS stepped T25→T20 (hit 28), Tatum PTS stepped T20→T15 (hit 17), Embiid PTS stepped T25→T20→T15 (hit 19), Randle PTS stepped T15→T10 (hit 18), George PTS stepped T15→T10 (hit 23), Brown 3PM held at T1 (hit 2). The mechanical enforcement of FG_MARGIN flags delivered a clean floor-protection record across all flagged PTS props except Brown PTS T20 and Murray PTS T15.

The 70-75% confidence band outperformed expected rate by +8.7pp (81.2% actual vs 72.5% expected), continuing to validate that conservative tier selection in this band is well-calibrated — the Analyst is not under-picking in this range. The system's floor-confidence discipline is generating alpha.

The Maxey iron_floor + consistent tag at T15 PTS (stepped from T20 via FG_MARGIN_THIN) hit at 30 points — more than double the pick value. The home elimination game context and the established PHI offensive identity around Maxey as the primary engine were correctly identified as reinforcing factors. The pick logic of honoring the iron_floor signal even after a step-down was validated.

3PM props went 3/3 (100%) across Tatum T2, Brown T1, and Cameron Johnson T1. All three correctly applied the 80% non-iron-floor cap and used the consistent/trend signals appropriately. The trend=down penalty on Johnson T1 (held at T1 with -5% confidence) was the right mechanical call — he hit for 5 threes, far above threshold.

REB props went 9/9 (100%) across all confidence bands — the tier-walk discipline on rebounding (25th-percentile gate, offensive-first extra strictness, 3rd-lowest floor check) is working exactly as designed. Every REB pick cleared its threshold, many by wide margins (Mobley 14 vs T6, Barnes 7 vs T4, Thompson 10 vs T6). Continue applying these gates without relaxation.

Stepped-down PTS picks (FG_MARGIN_THIN and FG_MARGIN_NEG triggers) were uniformly correct: Bane T10 hit 17, Barnes T10 hit 25, Barrett T10 hit 24, Mitchell T15 hit 24, Harden T10 hit 16. The mandatory step-down cascade for margin signals is protecting pick quality — these all would have been more vulnerable at T15+. The mechanism is calibrated correctly.

The Harden AST T4 'bulletproof' dossier signal held again: 5/5 series, actual 9 AST vs pick 4. The H31 within-series stability framework for Harden AST is the strongest single-player dossier signal in the system. The analyst correctly applied vs_tough=100% to negate the tough opponent penalty and correctly excluded late_fader from AST. This two-step reasoning should be preserved as a template.

The 70-75% confidence band significantly outperformed expectation (86.7% actual vs 72.5% expected), continuing a trend of the system's most conservative picks being its most reliable. The tier-walk discipline — stepping down via FG_MARGIN flags, floor gates, and series data — is generating picks that land well inside safe thresholds rather than at the edge. The minimum confidence floor architecture is working.

Injury-resilient pick construction worked well: Jabari Smith Jr. REB T6 hit 12 despite his ankle injury exit (post_game_event = injury_exit) because the pick threshold (6) was low enough that he cleared it before leaving the game. The tier-walk discipline of stepping to conservative floors provides genuine injury buffer on lower-tier picks — this is a structural feature, not luck.
recent_recommendations: Institute a hard skip on Jokic REB T12 when MIN×rebounding competition (specifically Gobert at full health) is present in the same game and the VOLATILE tag is active. The G5 16-AST pick demonstrated that Jokic's playmaking overrides the MIN×AST suppressor in competitive scripts — but his rebounding is structurally competed by Gobert in a way the quant H2H signal does not isolate. Add a rebounding_competition_flag check: if opponent center avg REB ≥ 10.0 in series and VOLATILE tag is present, apply an additional -5pp penalty or require 10/10 H2H rate at the target tier (not 9/10) before selecting Jokic REB T12+.

Apply the CLV ast_lost_close warning signal (-0.5pp threshold) as a mandatory confidence penalty of -3pp on AST props where the warning fires, rather than the current 'applied: false' status. Four AST picks this slate (Murray AST T4, Maxey AST T6, Brunson AST T4, White AST T2) all had active CLV warnings with penalties not applied. Three of those four hit, but White AST T2 missed — and the warning was the only live pre-game signal that the market had already downgraded the probability. Applying a -3pp mechanical penalty would reduce confidence without flipping any pick to skip, while creating an accurate record of market disagreement.

For Murray PTS props: when both FG_COLD (≥15%) AND FG_MARGIN_THIN/NEG fire simultaneously on a VOLATILE player, treat the double-step cascade (T25→T20→T15) as the maximum selection floor, and require series hit rate at the walked tier to be ≥80% (not just 70%) before selecting. Murray's series 5/5 at T15 was the justifying signal, but his actual output (12 pts in 40 min) shows that even T15 was too aggressive when both shooting suppression flags are simultaneously active. A combined-flag threshold of 80% series hit rate would have kept Murray at T15 as a borderline pass but surfaced the risk more explicitly.

Brown REB T4 is a case where the PHI system-wide suppressor's mechanism — scheme-based disruption of offensive flow — plausibly compresses non-primary-scorer rebounding through fewer BOS offensive possessions and disrupted rhythm. Add a cross-prop PHI suppressor note: for BOS secondary contributors (non-Tatum, non-Brown primary) at REB T4 in PHI suppressor context, apply an additional -3pp penalty to reflect that PHI's scheme reduces BOS possessions generally, not only scoring actions. This would not have changed the Brown REB pick direction (T4 stayed selected) but would have appropriately reduced confidence from 76% to ~73%, keeping it in the lower band.

For any pick where the CLV ast_lost_close warning fires AND the morning_implied_prob is ≥90% (a very high market consensus price), treat this combination as a mandatory 'FADE' override regardless of the Analyst's calibrated_edge calculation — even if calibrated_edge is positive. Murray AST T4 (morning 94.7%, market 94.1%, CLV -0.62pp) and White AST T2 (morning 96.2%, market 93.3%, CLV -2.82pp) both had morning implied probs above 93% with CLV moving against the pick. When the market prices a prop above 93% and then moves further against it, the pick has near-zero positive EV even if the hit-rate quant supports it. Automate a FADE recommendation (not skip from the slate, but flag for the bet_recommendation engine) when morning_implied_prob ≥ 93% AND clv_warning.live_clv_pp < -0.5.

Harden 3PM playoff CAUTION: Upgrade from advisory to mechanical. Apply a mandatory tier-step-down on Harden 3PM in all playoff games (T2→T1, T3→T2). The cross-season dossier shows 5/5 prior playoffs below RS career with a worsening trajectory. The 5/5 series hit rate at T2 was a within-series signal that conflicted with the multi-season structural suppression — within-series 3PM data should not override a 5-season directional CAUTION for Harden. Add a specific rule: 'Harden 3PM in playoffs: max pick tier = T1 unless iron_floor present.'

AST picks for the losing team in projected blowouts require additional scrutiny. Add a rule: when the pre-game spread implies a potential blowout (abs spread ≥ 8) and the AST pick is for a player on the projected losing team, apply an additional -5pp penalty or require a step-down from the primary AST tier. The LAL blowout over HOU (final margin 20) compressed Thompson's assist sequences in exactly the pattern the game-script framework predicts. The CLV warning for Thompson AST (-2.21pp live CLV) was also available and unapplied — consider activating the proposed H34 CLV penalty for AST picks when live CLV disagrees by ≥ 1.5pp.

For 3PM picks carrying a cross-season playoff CAUTION tag (Harden, McCollum per dossier), the non-iron-floor 80% confidence cap should be lowered to 75% and the pick should require a minimum hit rate of 8/10 at the selected tier in the recent L10 window, not 7/10. The 5-game series window alone is insufficient to override a multi-season suppression trajectory — require confirmation across a longer window or step down unconditionally.

The CLV warning mechanism (H34 backtest_clv_ast_disagree) fired on both Barnes AST T6 and Thompson AST T4 with live CLV losses of -0.8pp and -2.21pp respectively. Barnes AST hit (14 actual vs 6 pick) so the warning was overcautious there, but Thompson AST missed. Consider activating the proposed 5pp penalty for AST picks when live CLV drops ≥ 1.5pp from morning line — this threshold would have fired on Thompson (-2.21pp) but not Barnes (-0.8pp), providing a tighter filter. The Barnes case confirms the 0.5pp threshold is too sensitive; 1.5pp is a better activation point.

Jabari Smith Jr. had three simultaneous picks (3PM, PTS, REB) in the same game with an injury exit result. The REB pick hit (12 actual vs T6) before he left, but the lower-tier PTS T10 missed (9 actual vs 10 pick — a 1-unit miss) and 3PM T2 missed. When a player has 3+ picks on the same slate and carries FG_MARGIN signals plus a 'stable/down' trend on PTS, consider capping exposure to 2 props maximum (prioritizing REB iron_floor type picks and skipping the marginal PTS/3PM at low confidence). Concentrated single-player exposure amplifies injury and variance risk across multiple prop types simultaneously.
